---
title: "DSC 011 S26 Lecture NN Demo (KEY)"
subtitle: "Bash in Quarto — Setup Reference"
author: "FirstName LastName"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        pre.stdout {
          background: #f8f8f8;
          border-left: 3px solid #4caf50;
          padding: 0.5em 1em;
          margin-bottom: 0.2em;
          font-family: monospace;
          white-space: pre-wrap;
        }
        pre.stderr {
          background: #fff3f3;
          border-left: 3px solid #e53935;
          color: #b71c1c;
          padding: 0.5em 1em;
          margin-top: 0.2em;
          font-family: monospace;
          white-space: pre-wrap;
        }
        .check-correct   { color: #2e7d32; font-weight: bold; }
        .check-incorrect { color: #c62828; font-weight: bold; }
        </style>
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
engine: knitr
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE, cache=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results="hide"}

# ── Standard knitr options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
options(digits = 5)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo     = TRUE,
  cache    = TRUE,
  warning  = FALSE,
  message  = FALSE,
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment  = ""          # removes ## prefix from all chunk output
)

# ── Fix PATH for bash chunks on macOS ────────────────────────────────────────
# Problem: RStudio launched from the Dock inherits a minimal system PATH from
# launchd that does not include /opt/homebrew/bin. knitr bash chunks therefore
# use /bin/bash 3.2 (Apple's GPL-frozen version) instead of Homebrew bash 5.
#
# This block prepends Homebrew paths into the R session PATH so knitr passes
# the correct PATH to bash chunks. It is safe on Linux/WSL2 — non-existent
# paths are ignored.
#
# This is Fix 2 of 4 in the layered defence:
#   Fix 1 (terminal): Launch RStudio from terminal: open -a RStudio
#   Fix 2 (notebook): This setup chunk — patches PATH inside the R session
#   Fix 3 (notebook): bash-check chunk below — fails loudly if bash < 4
#   Fix 4 (system):   /etc/paths.d/homebrew — permanent system-wide fix
#
# Students should apply Fix 4 once during system setup. Fix 1 is good
# practice regardless. Fixes 2 and 3 are belt-and-suspenders in notebooks.

homebrew_paths <- c(
  "/opt/homebrew/bin",   # Apple Silicon Macs (M1/M2/M3/M4)
  "/usr/local/bin"       # Intel Macs
)
existing <- homebrew_paths[dir.exists(homebrew_paths)]
if (length(existing) > 0) {
  old_path <- Sys.getenv("PATH")
  new_path <- paste(c(existing, old_path), collapse = ":")
  Sys.setenv(PATH = new_path)
}

# ── R print_and_check (v2.2) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
print_and_check <- function(answer, checkstring) {
  print(answer)
  check <- ifelse(digest::digest(answer) == checkstring,
                  "✓ CORRECT",
                  "✗ INCORRECT")
  print(check)
}

# ── Custom knitr engine: styled-bash ─────────────────────────────────────────
# Captures stdout and stderr separately and renders them as styled HTML divs:
#   stdout → green left border
#   stderr → red left border, labelled [stderr]
#
# Use {styled-bash} when stderr distinction matters in rendered output.
# Use plain {bash} for most chunks — comment="" already removes ## prefix.

knitr::knit_engines$set(`styled-bash` = function(options) {
  code <- paste(options$code, collapse = "\n")

  tmp_out <- tempfile()
  tmp_err <- tempfile()
  on.exit({ unlink(tmp_out); unlink(tmp_err) })

  system2("bash",
    args   = c("-c", shQuote(code)),
    stdout = tmp_out,
    stderr = tmp_err
  )

  stdout_txt <- paste(readLines(tmp_out, warn = FALSE), collapse = "\n")
  stderr_txt <- paste(readLines(tmp_err, warn = FALSE), collapse = "\n")

  html_parts <- character(0)

  if (nzchar(stdout_txt)) {
    stdout_html <- gsub("✓ CORRECT",
      '<span class="check-correct">✓ CORRECT</span>', stdout_txt, fixed = TRUE)
    stdout_html <- gsub("✗ INCORRECT",
      '<span class="check-incorrect">✗ INCORRECT</span>', stdout_html, fixed = TRUE)
    html_parts <- c(html_parts,
      paste0('<pre class="stdout">', stdout_html, '</pre>'))
  }

  if (nzchar(stderr_txt)) {
    html_parts <- c(html_parts,
      paste0('<pre class="stderr"><strong>[stderr]</strong>\n',
             htmltools::htmlEscape(stderr_txt), '</pre>'))
  }

  html_out <- paste(html_parts, collapse = "\n")
  knitr::engine_output(options, options$code, out = list(
    structure(list(src = html_out), class = c("knit_asis", "knit_asis_url"))
  ))
})
```

## Bash Environment Check

**Always include this chunk immediately after the setup chunk in every
bash-using notebook.** It verifies that knitr is using bash 4+ and gives
students a clear, actionable error message if not. The chunk intentionally
uses only POSIX-compatible bash 3.2 syntax so it runs even before the
problem is fixed.

```{bash bash-check}
BASH_MAJOR="${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}"
if [[ "$BASH_MAJOR" -lt 4 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: bash $BASH_VERSION is too old for this notebook."
    echo ""
    echo "Fix options — apply in order, restart RStudio after each:"
    echo "  Fix 1: Launch RStudio from your terminal:"
    echo "           open -a RStudio"
    echo "  Fix 2: Add Homebrew bash to /etc/paths.d/ (see course setup notes)"
    echo "           echo /opt/homebrew/bin | sudo tee /etc/paths.d/homebrew"
    echo "  Fix 3: Confirm brew bash is installed:"
    echo "           brew install bash"
    exit 1
fi
echo "✓ bash $BASH_VERSION"
echo "✓ $(which bash)"
echo "✓ PATH includes: $(echo "$PATH" | tr ':' '\n' | grep -E 'homebrew|local' | head -3 | tr '\n' ' ')"
```

---

## macOS System Setup Instructions (Fix 4)

Include these instructions in your course setup materials. Students run
them once during initial system configuration.

### What and why

macOS ships bash 3.2 at `/bin/bash` and will never update it (Apple
froze it at GPL v2). Homebrew installs bash 5 at `/opt/homebrew/bin/bash`.

GUI apps like RStudio launched from the Dock inherit PATH from `launchd`,
not from your shell configuration, so they don't see `/opt/homebrew/bin`
even if your terminal does. The permanent fix is to add Homebrew's bin
directory to `/etc/paths.d/`, which `path_helper` reads for all processes
including GUI apps.

### Student instructions (macOS only)

```bash
# Step 1: Install Homebrew bash 5 if not already installed
brew install bash
/opt/homebrew/bin/bash --version   # should show 5.x

# Step 2: Add Homebrew bin to system-wide PATH via /etc/paths.d/
# This makes /opt/homebrew/bin available to ALL processes including
# GUI apps like RStudio, even when launched from the Dock.
echo /opt/homebrew/bin | sudo tee /etc/paths.d/homebrew

# Step 3: Verify the file was created correctly
cat /etc/paths.d/homebrew          # should print: /opt/homebrew/bin

# Step 4: Restart your Mac (or at minimum, fully quit and reopen RStudio)

# Step 5: Verify RStudio sees the correct bash by rendering a notebook
# with the bash-check chunk above — it should show bash 5.x
```

### How /etc/paths.d/ works

`/etc/paths.d/` is a directory of plain-text files, each containing one
path per line. macOS's `path_helper` utility reads all files in this
directory at login and prepends them to PATH for every process — including
GUI apps. This is different from `~/.zshrc` or `~/.bash_profile`, which
only apply to interactive terminal sessions.

```bash
# Inspect all paths.d entries on your system:
ls /etc/paths.d/
cat /etc/paths

# See what path_helper produces:
/usr/libexec/path_helper
```

### WSL2/Ubuntu note

WSL2 users do not need any of these steps. Ubuntu ships bash 5.x at
`/usr/bin/bash` and RStudio Server (or RStudio Desktop on WSL2) inherits
the correct PATH automatically.

---

## Usage Patterns

### Pattern 1 — Plain bash chunk (most common)

```{bash demo-plain}
echo "The current date is: $(date)"
wc -l /etc/hosts
```

### Pattern 2 — Pipe into check_bash

```{bash demo-pipe}
# Generate this hash with: echo "hello world" | check_bash --make-key
echo "hello world" | check_bash "b94d27b9934d3e08a52e52d7da7dabfac484efe04681d8714cc940430e5fa9f"
```

### Pattern 3 — Capture then check (mirrors R's answer <- ... pattern)

```{bash demo-capture}
answer=$(echo "hello world")
check_bash "$answer" "b94d27b9934d3e08a52e52d7da7dabfac484efe04681d8714cc940430e5fa9f"
```

### Pattern 4 — Combined output + code structure check

```{bash demo-combined}
MY_CODE='echo hello | tr a-z A-Z'
eval "$MY_CODE" | check_bash \
  --code "$MY_CODE" \
  --requires tr \
  --forbid awk \
  --pipeline 2 \
  "HASH_FROM_KEY_HERE"
```

### Pattern 5 — File checking

```{bash demo-file}
echo "expected output" > /tmp/student_result.txt
check_bash --file /tmp/student_result.txt "HASH_FROM_KEY_HERE"
```

### Pattern 6 — Styled stderr engine

```{styled-bash demo-stderr}
ls /this/does/not/exist
echo "This goes to stdout"
```

---

## Instructor Key Generation

Set `eval: false` on key-generation chunks so they don't run when
students render the notebook:

```{bash key-gen}
#| eval: false

# Output hash:
echo "hello world" | check_bash --make-key

# File hash:
check_bash --file expected_output.csv --make-key

# Capture hash:
answer=$(wc -l < /etc/hosts)
check_bash --make-key "$answer"

# Normalized hash (students must also use the -n flag):
some_command | check_bash -n --make-key
```
